declarations.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-ai-button` component serves as a button for AI-related scenarios. Users can trigger actions by clicking or tapping the `ui5-ai-button`
or by pressing keyboard keys like [Enter] or [Space].

### Usage

For the `ui5-ai-button` user interface, you can define one or more button states by placing `ui5-ai-button-state` components in their default slot.
Each state has a name for identification and can include text, an icon, and an end icon, as needed for its purpose.
You can define a split mode for the `ui5-ai-button`, which will results in displaying an arrow button for additional actions.

You can choose from a set of predefined designs for `ui5-ai-button` (as in `ui5-button`) to match the desired styling.

The `ui5-ai-button` can be activated by clicking or tapping it. You can change the button state in the click event handler. When the button is
in split mode, you can activate the default button action by clicking or tapping it, or by pressing keyboard keys like [Enter] or [Space].
You can activate the arrow button by clicking or tapping it, or by pressing keyboard keys like [Arrow Up], [Arrow Down], or [F4].
To display additional actions, you can attach a menu to the arrow button.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-ai/dist/Button.js";`
declarations.name: Button

slots.name: default
slots.description:
Defines the available states of the component.
**Note:** Although this slot accepts HTML Elements, it is strongly recommended that
you only use `ui5-ai-button-state` components in order to preserve the intended design.

members.name: accessibilityAttributes
members.description:
Defines the additional accessibility attributes that will be applied to the component.

This property allows for fine-tuned control of ARIA attributes for screen reader support.
It accepts an object with the following optional fields:

- **root**: Accessibility attributes that will be applied to the root element.
 - **hasPopup**: Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element (such as a menu or dialog).
   Accepts string values: `"dialog"`, `"grid"`, `"listbox"`, `"menu"`, or `"tree"`.
 - **roleDescription**: Defines a human-readable description for the button's role.
   Accepts any string value.
 - **title**: Specifies a tooltip or description for screen readers.
   Accepts any string value.
- **ariaKeyShortcuts**: Defines keyboard shortcuts that activate or focus the button.

- **arrowButton**: Accessibility attributes that will be applied to the arrow (split) button element.
 - **hasPopup**: Indicates the type of popup triggered by the arrow button.
   Accepts string values: `"dialog"`, `"grid"`, `"listbox"`, `"menu"`, or `"tree"`.
 - **expanded**: Indicates whether the popup controlled by the arrow button is currently expanded.
   Accepts boolean values: `true` or `false`.

members.name: arrowButtonPressed
members.description:
Defines the active state of the arrow button in split mode.
Set to true when the button is in split mode and a menu with additional options
is opened by the arrow button. Set back to false when the menu is closed.

members.name: design
members.description: Defines the component design.

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Defines whether the component is disabled.
A disabled component can't be pressed or
focused, and it is not in the tab chain.

members.name: state
members.description: Defines the current state of the component.

events.name: arrow-button-click
events.description:
Fired when the component is in split mode and after the arrow button
is activated either by clicking or tapping it or by using the [Arrow Up] / [Arrow Down],
[Alt] + [Arrow Up]/ [Arrow Down], or [F4] keyboard keys.

events.name: click
events.description:
Fired when the component is activated either with a
mouse/tap or by using the Enter or Space key.

attributes.description:
Defines the additional accessibility attributes that will be applied to the component.

This property allows for fine-tuned control of ARIA attributes for screen reader support.
It accepts an object with the following optional fields:

- **root**: Accessibility attributes that will be applied to the root element.
 - **hasPopup**: Indicates the availability and type of interactive popup element (such as a menu or dialog).
   Accepts string values: `"dialog"`, `"grid"`, `"listbox"`, `"menu"`, or `"tree"`.
 - **roleDescription**: Defines a human-readable description for the button's role.
   Accepts any string value.
 - **title**: Specifies a tooltip or description for screen readers.
   Accepts any string value.
- **ariaKeyShortcuts**: Defines keyboard shortcuts that activate or focus the button.

- **arrowButton**: Accessibility attributes that will be applied to the arrow (split) button element.
 - **hasPopup**: Indicates the type of popup triggered by the arrow button.
   Accepts string values: `"dialog"`, `"grid"`, `"listbox"`, `"menu"`, or `"tree"`.
 - **expanded**: Indicates whether the popup controlled by the arrow button is currently expanded.
   Accepts boolean values: `true` or `false`.
attributes.name: accessibility-attributes

attributes.description:
Defines the active state of the arrow button in split mode.
Set to true when the button is in split mode and a menu with additional options
is opened by the arrow button. Set back to false when the menu is closed.

attributes.description: Defines the component design.

Defines whether the component is disabled.
A disabled component can't be pressed or
focused, and it is not in the tab chain.

The **@ui5/webcomponents-ai** package (including Button and ButtonState) is under active development and considered experimental. Component APIs are subject to change.
Furthermore, the package supports **Horizon** themes only.

declarations.description:
### Overview

`ui5-ai-button-state` is the item to use for defining states of `ui5-ai-button` components.

### Usage

`ui5-ai-button-state` is an abstract element, representing a state of `ui5-ai-button`. It is meant to be used in the `default` slot
of `ui5-ai-button` and should not be used as standalone component.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents/dist/AiButtonState.js";`
declarations.name: ButtonState

members.name: endIcon
members.description:
Defines the icon to be displayed as graphical element within the component after the text.
The SAP-icons font provides numerous options.

**Example:**

See all the available icons in the [Icon Explorer](https://sdk.openui5.org/test-resources/sap/m/demokit/iconExplorer/webapp/index.html).

declarations.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-ai-input` component extends the standard `ui5-input` with **AI Writing Assistant** capabilities.

### Structure

The `ui5-ai-input` consists of the following main parts:

- **Input Field** – Inherits all standard Input behaviors.
- **AI Action Button** – Appears when focused or loading, providing access to AI-related actions or stopping generation.

The component automatically determines which elements to render based on its internal state:
- The AI Button is only shown when there are available `actions`.
- The version navigation appears only when `totalVersions > 1`.

### Keyboard Support

- **Shift + F4** — Opens the AI menu.
- **Ctrl + Shift + Z / Y** — Navigates backward/forward between AI-generated versions.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-ai/dist/Input.js";`
declarations.name: Input

slots.name: actions
slots.description: Defines the items of the menu for the component.

slots.name: default
slots.description:
Defines the suggestion items.

**Note:** The suggestions would be displayed only if the `showSuggestions`
property is set to `true`.

**Note:** The `<ui5-suggestion-item>`, `<ui5-suggestion-item-group>` and `ui5-suggestion-item-custom` are recommended to be used as suggestion items.

slots.name: icon
slots.description: Defines the icon to be displayed in the component.

slots.name: valueStateMessage
slots.description:
Defines the value state message that will be displayed as pop up under the component.
The value state message slot should contain only one root element.

**Note:** If not specified, a default text (in the respective language) will be displayed.

**Note:** The `valueStateMessage` would be displayed,
when the component is in `Information`, `Critical` or `Negative` value state.

**Note:** If the component has `suggestionItems`,
the `valueStateMessage` would be displayed as part of the same popover, if used on desktop, or dialog - on phone.

members.name: currentVersion
members.description: Indicates the index of the currently displayed version.

members.name: loading
members.description:
Defines whether the AI Writing Assistant is currently loading.

When `true`, indicates that an AI action is in progress.

members.name: totalVersions
members.description:
Indicates the total number of result versions available.

When not set or set to 0, the versioning will be hidden.

members.name: accessibleDescription
members.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

members.name: accessibleDescriptionRef
members.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that describe the input.

members.name: accessibleName
members.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

members.name: accessibleNameRef
members.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the input.

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Defines whether the component is in disabled state.

**Note:** A disabled component is completely noninteractive.

members.name: filter
members.description: Defines the filter type of the component.

members.name: maxlength
members.description:
Sets the maximum number of characters available in the input field.

**Note:** This property is not compatible with the ui5-input type InputType.Number. If the ui5-input type is set to Number, the maxlength value is ignored.

members.name: name
members.description:
Determines the name by which the component will be identified upon submission in an HTML form.

**Note:** This property is only applicable within the context of an HTML Form element.

members.name: noTypeahead
members.description: Defines whether the value will be autcompleted to match an item

members.name: open
members.description:
Defines whether the suggestions picker is open.
The picker will not open if the `showSuggestions` property is set to `false`, the input is disabled or the input is readonly.
The picker will close automatically and `close` event will be fired if the input is not in the viewport.

members.name: placeholder
members.description:
Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the
component has no value.

members.name: readonly
members.description:
Defines whether the component is read-only.

**Note:** A read-only component is not editable,
but still provides visual feedback upon user interaction.

members.name: required
members.description: Defines whether the component is required.

members.name: showClearIcon
members.description: Defines whether the clear icon of the input will be shown.

members.name: showSuggestions
members.description: Defines whether the component should show suggestions, if such are present.

members.name: type
members.description:
Defines the HTML type of the component.

**Notes:**

- The particular effect of this property differs depending on the browser
and the current language settings, especially for type `Number`.
- The property is mostly intended to be used with touch devices
that use different soft keyboard layouts depending on the given input type.

members.name: value
members.description:
Defines the value of the component.

**Note:** The property is updated upon typing.

members.name: valueState
members.description: Defines the value state of the component.

events.name: button-click
events.description: Fired when the user selects the AI button.

events.name: item-click
events.description: Fired when an item from the AI actions menu is clicked.

_ui5parameters.name: text
_ui5parameters.description: The text of the currently clicked menu item.

events.name: stop-generation
events.description: Fired when the user selects the "Stop" button to stop ongoing AI text generation.

events.name: version-change
events.description: Fired when the user selects the version navigation buttons.

_ui5parameters.name: backwards
_ui5parameters.description: The text of the currently clicked menu item.

and when a suggestion item has been selected.

events.name: selection-change
events.description:
Fired when the user navigates to a suggestion item via the ARROW keys,
as a preview, before the final selection.

declarations.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-ai-prompt-input` component allows the user to write custom instructions in natural language, so that AI is guided to generate content tailored to user needs.

**Note:** The web component is in an experimental state

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-ai/dist/PromptInput.js"`
declarations.name: PromptInput

slots.description:
Defines the suggestion items.

**Note:** The suggestions would be displayed only if the `showSuggestions`
property is set to `true`.

**Note:** The `<ui5-suggestion-item>`, `<ui5-suggestion-item-group>` and `ui5-suggestion-item-custom` are recommended to be used as suggestion items.

Defines the value state message that will be displayed as pop up under the component.
The value state message slot should contain only one root element.

**Note:** If not specified, a default text (in the respective language) will be displayed.

**Note:** The `valueStateMessage` would be displayed,
when the component is in `Information`, `Critical` or `Negative` value state.

members.description:
Determines whether the characters exceeding the maximum allowed character count are visible
in the component.

If set to `false`, the user is not allowed to enter more characters than what is set in the
`maxlength` property.
If set to `true` the characters exceeding the `maxlength` value are selected on
paste and the counter below the component displays their number.

declarations.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-ai-textarea` component extends the standard TextArea with Writing Assistant capabilities.
It provides AI-powered text generation, editing suggestions, and version management functionality.

### Structure
The `ui5-ai-textarea` consists of the following elements:
- TextArea: The main text input area with all standard textarea functionality
- WritingAssistant: Dedicated toolbar containing:
  - Versioning: A component with left/right navigation buttons and a label for browsing AI-generated versions
  - AI Button: Opens a menu that can be extended with custom AI generation options through slotting

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-ai/dist/TextArea.js";`
declarations.name: TextArea

slots.description: Defines a slot for `ui5-menu` integration. This slot allows you to pass a `ui5-menu` instance that will be associated with the assistant.
slots.name: menu
valueStateMessage
slots.description:
Defines the value state message that will be displayed as pop up under the component.
The value state message slot should contain only one root element.
 
**Note:** If not specified, a default text (in the respective language) will be displayed.

**Note:** The `valueStateMessage` would be displayed if the component has
`valueState` of type `Information`, `Critical` or `Negative`.

members.name: currentVersion
members.description: Indicates the index of the currently displayed version.

members.name: loading
members.description: Defines whether the `ui5-ai-textarea` is currently in a loading(processing) state.

members.name: promptDescription
members.description: Defines the prompt description of the current action.

members.name: totalVersions
members.description:
Indicates the total number of result versions available.

Notes:
Versioning is hidden if the value is `0`

members.name: accessibleDescription
members.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

members.name: accessibleDescriptionRef
members.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that describe the textarea.

members.name: accessibleName
members.description: Defines the accessible ARIA name of the component.

members.name: accessibleNameRef
members.description: Receives id(or many ids) of the elements that label the textarea.

members.name: disabled
members.description:
Indicates whether the user can interact with the component or not.

**Note:** A disabled component is completely noninteractive.

members.name: growing
members.description: Enables the component to automatically grow and shrink dynamically with its content.

members.name: growingMaxRows
members.description: Defines the maximum number of rows that the component can grow.

members.name: maxlength
members.description: Defines the maximum number of characters that the `value` can have.

members.name: name
members.description:
Determines the name by which the component will be identified upon submission in an HTML form.

**Note:** This property is only applicable within the context of an HTML Form element.

members.name: placeholder
members.description: Defines a short hint intended to aid the user with data entry when the component has no value.

members.name: readonly
members.description:
Defines whether the component is read-only.

**Note:** A read-only component is not editable,
but still provides visual feedback upon user interaction.

members.name: required
members.description: Defines whether the component is required.

members.name: rows
members.description:
Defines the number of visible text rows for the component.

**Notes:**

- If the `growing` property is enabled, this property defines the minimum rows to be displayed
in the textarea.
- The CSS `height` property wins over the `rows` property, if both are set.

members.name: showExceededText
members.description:
Determines whether the characters exceeding the maximum allowed character count are visible
in the component.

If set to `false`, the user is not allowed to enter more characters than what is set in the
`maxlength` property.
If set to `true` the characters exceeding the `maxlength` value are selected on
paste and the counter below the component displays their number.

members.name: value
members.description: Defines the value of the component.

members.name: valueState
members.description:
Defines the value state of the component.

**Note:** If `maxlength` property is set,
the component turns into "Critical" state once the characters exceeds the limit.
In this case, only the "Negative" state is considered and can be applied.

events.name: stop-generation
events.description: Fired when the user requests to stop AI text generation.

events.name: version-change
events.description: Fired when the user clicks on version navigation buttons.

_ui5parameters.name: backwards
_ui5parameters.description: Indicates if navigation is backwards (true) or forwards (false, default).

events.name: change
events.description: Fired when the text has changed and the focus leaves the component.

events.name: input
events.description:
Fired when the value of the component changes at each keystroke or when
something is pasted.

_ui5parameters.name: escapePressed
_ui5parameters.description: Indicates whether the Escape key was pressed, which triggers a revert to the previous value

events.name: scroll
events.description: Fired when textarea is scrolled.

events.name: select
events.description: Fired when some text has been selected.

**Notes:**

- If the `growing` property is enabled, this property defines the minimum rows to be displayed
in the textarea.
- The CSS `height` property wins over the `rows` property, if both are set.
attributes.name: rows

attributes.description:
Determines whether the characters exceeding the maximum allowed character count are visible
in the component.

If set to `false`, the user is not allowed to enter more characters than what is set in the
`maxlength` property.
If set to `true` the characters exceeding the `maxlength` value are selected on
paste and the counter below the component displays their number.
attributes.name: show-exceeded-text

attributes.description: Defines the value of the component.
attributes.name: value

attributes.description:
Defines the value state of the component.

**Note:** If `maxlength` property is set,
the component turns into "Critical" state once the characters exceeds the limit.
In this case, only the "Negative" state is considered and can be applied.
attributes.name: value-state

declarations._ui5experimental:
The **@ui5/webcomponents-ai** package is under development and considered experimental - components' APIs are subject to change.
Furthermore, the package supports **Horizon** themes only.

cssParts.description: Used to style the native textarea
cssParts.name: textarea

declarations.description:
### Overview
The `ui5-ai-toolbar-label` represents a text label,
used in the `ui5-toolbar`.

### ES6 Module Import
`import "@ui5/webcomponents-ai/dist/ToolbarLabel.js";`
declarations.name: ToolbarLabel

members.name: text
members.description: Defines the text of the label.

members.name: overflowPriority
members.description:
Property used to define the access of the item to the overflow Popover. If "NeverOverflow" option is set,
the item never goes in the Popover, if "AlwaysOverflow" - it never comes out of it.

members.name: preventOverflowClosing
members.description:
Defines if the toolbar overflow popup should close upon interaction with the item.
It will close by default.

declarations.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-ai-versioning` component provides navigation controls for AI-generated content versions.
It displays the current version index and total versions, with previous/next navigation buttons.
This component extends ToolbarItem to participate in toolbar overflow behavior as a single unit.

### Structure
The `ui5-ai-versioning` consists of the following elements:
- Previous Button: Navigates to the previous version (disabled when at first version)
- Version Counter: Shows current version / total versions (e.g., "2 / 5")
- Next Button: Navigates to the nex
t version (disabled when at last version)

### Focus Management
The component automatically manages focus when users reach version boundaries,
moving focus to the available navigation button when one becomes disabled.

### Responsive Behavior
When used in a toolbar, the entire versioning component (buttons + label) will overflow
together as a single unit when there is insufficient space.

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@ui5/webcomponents-ai/dist/Versioning.js";`
declarations.name: Versioning

members.name: currentStep
members.description:
Indicates the index of the currently displayed result version.

This property represents the current position in the version history.

members.name: totalSteps
members.description:
The total number of available result versions.

Note: Versioning is hidden if the value is `0`.

members.name: overflowPriority
members.description:
Property used to define the access of the item to the overflow Popover. If "NeverOverflow" option is set,
the item never goes in the Popover, if "AlwaysOverflow" - it never comes out of it.

members.name: preventOverflowClosing
members.description:
Defines if the toolbar overflow popup should close upon interaction with the item.
It will close by default.

events.name: version-change
events.description: Fired when the user clicks on version navigation buttons.

This property represents the current position in the version history.

Note: Versioning is hidden if the value is `0`.

attributes.description:
Property used to define the access of the item to the overflow Popover. If "NeverOverflow" option is set,
the item never goes in the Popover, if "AlwaysOverflow" - it never comes out of it.
attributes.name: overflow-priority

attributes.description:
Defines if the toolbar overflow popup should close upon interaction with the item.
It will close by default.

declarations.description:
### Overview

The `ui5-ai-textarea-toolbar` component provides a specialized toolbar for AI TextArea functionality.
It manages different states of the AI assistant and provides version navigation capabilities.

### Structure
The `ui5-ai-textarea-toolbar` consists of the following elements:
- AI Generate Button: Triggers AI text generation or stops ongoing generation
- Version Navigation: Allows navigation between multiple AI-generated results
- Action Label: Displays the current AI action being performed

### ES6 Module Import

`import "@sap-webcomponents/ai/dist/WritingAssistant.js";`
declarations.name: WritingAssistant

members.name: currentVersion
members.description:
Indicates the index of the currently displayed result version.

The index is **0-based** (i.e. `0` represents the first result).
This property is synchronized with the parent AI TextArea component.

members.name: handleButtonClick
members.description:
Handles the click event for the AI generate button.
Toggles between generate and stop states based on current button state.

members.name: promptDescription
members.description:
Defines the prompt description of the Writing Assistant.

This text is displayed in the toolbar to indicate the current or last
performed AI action (e.g., "Generated text", "Simplified text").

members.name: totalVersions
members.description:
Indicates the total number of result versions available.

This property determines whether version navigation controls are displayed.
When totalVersions > 0, previous/next buttons become available.

events.name: button-click
events.description: Fired when the user clicks on the AI button.

events.name: stop-generation
events.description: Fired when the user clicks on the "Stop" button to stop ongoing AI text generation.

events.name: version-change
events.description: Fired when the user clicks on version navigation buttons.

attributes.description:
Indicates the index of the currently displayed result version.

The index is **0-based** (i.e. `0` represents the first result).
This property is synchronized with the parent AI TextArea component.
attributes.name: current-version

attributes.description:
Defines the prompt description of the Writing Assistant.

This text is displayed in the toolbar to indicate the current or last
performed AI action (e.g., "Generated text", "Simplified text").
attributes.name: prompt-description

attributes.description:
Indicates the total number of result versions available.

This property determines whether version navigation controls are displayed.
When totalVersions > 0, previous/next buttons become available.
attributes.name: total-versions
